Transformation Manager - job post

3.5

Longport ST5 9QD
 
Benefits
Pulled from the full job description

Employee discount
Free parking
Company pension
Canteen
Enhanced paternity leave
On-site parking
 
Full job description
We’re the feel-good fashion brand making style accessible and fun for over 55 years, on our website, mobile app and over 300 stores in the UK.

By living our values - we play to win, customer obsessed, we are one and it starts with me - we deliver That New Look Feeling for our customers and each other.

THE ROLE

As a Transformation Manager within our Distribution Centre, you will lead and deliver strategic and operational change initiatives that drive meaningful improvements across the site. You will focus on enhancing performance, efficiency, safety, cost effectiveness, and customer experience through process optimisation, the adoption of new technologies, and cultural development.
Acting as a vital link between strategy and day to day operations, you will ensure transformation activity is effectively implemented, fully embedded, and delivers sustainable long term benefits.

WHATS IN IT FOR YOU
Being part of the New Look Team means you get access to a great range of benefits & perks …
You’ll *love* our generous staff discount – 40% off for you and a loved one, and 25% off for up to 12 friends and family members!
Get special access to hundreds of discounts from top retailers and gyms, along with free workout classes, on our rewards platform Runway.
Plan for your retirement while you work with our contributory private pension scheme.
We’re all about celebrating our success, so we have a profit-related bonus scheme.
Our family-friendly policies include enhanced maternity, paternity and adoption leave, and shared parental leave.
Benefit from free Virtual GP access for you and your children – it allows you to speak to a doctor at a time and date that suits you.
Big trip on the cards? Unlock extra leave with our buy more holiday scheme.
Celebrate YOU! Enjoy an extra paid day off on your birthday each year.
Do your bit for the environment and save money with our Cycle2Work scheme.
Driving in? There’s free secure on-site parking available for your convenience.
Grab a bite at our on-site subsidised canteen.
We're proud to partner with the Retail Trust and Fashion & Textile Children's Trust
Please note – these benefits and perks are non-contractual and may be changed from time to time.

WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING (RESPONSIBILITIES)
Transformation & Change Delivery
Be instrumental in end-to-end transformation programmes across DC operations, including process redesign & operating model changes
Translate business strategy into practical, site-level transformation plans
Own and manage transformation roadmaps, milestones, and benefits tracking
Ensure change initiatives are embedded and sustained post-implementation
Continuous Improvement
Drive a culture of continuous improvement using Lean, Six Sigma, or similar methodologies
Identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities across inbound, outbound, inventory, and support functions
Facilitate workshops, value stream mapping, and problem-solving sessions
Coach operational leaders and teams in CI tools and behaviours
Operational Excellence
Partner with DC leadership to improve productivity, service, cost, and quality KPIs
Support peak readiness planning and post-peak reviews
Ensure changes align with health & safety, compliance, and regulatory requirements
Support standardisation of processes across shifts or multiple sites (if applicable)
Stakeholder Management
Work closely with Operations, Engineering, IT, HR, Finance, and central transformation teams
Influence senior stakeholders and operational leaders to gain buy-in and momentum
Manage third-party partners, consultants, or system vendors where required
People & Culture
Support leadership teams through change, building engagement and resilience
Champion a data-driven, customer-focused, and continuous improvement mindset
Develop transformation and CI capability within the DC

WHO YOU ARE (YOUR SKILLS/EXPERIEINCE)
Proven experience delivering transformation or change within a Distribution Centre, Logistics, or Supply Chain environment
Strong understanding of DC operations (inbound, outbound, inventory, labour planning)
Experience leading cross-functional change initiatives in a fast-paced operational setting
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
Ability to influence without direct authority
Lean / Six Sigma certification (Green Belt or above)
Experience with WMS implementations or upgrades
Exposure to automation or mechanisation projects
Project management qualification (PRINCE2, Agile, or similar)
Why New Look?

We care about you and the planet and believe fashion should be a force for positive change. We celebrate inclusion and diversity in everything we do. We’re proud of our inclusive culture and our talented team members who embrace our shared purpose, behaviours and values.

We prioritise development, offering training to support your progression, so you can be your absolute best and achieve your goals.

We pride ourselves on being a flexible employer, our colleagues work a range of patterns. If you have a specific pattern in mind, we're keen to discuss this with you in line with the output needed for the role.

Please ensure that your CV is in simple format e.g. Microsoft Word when applying using your CV to ensure smooth application process
shortCompanyReason: Fashion brand with 300+ UK stores and digital channels.
shortRoleReason: Lead transformation and continuous improvement in a distribution centre.
companySummary: New Look is a fashion retailer operating online, via mobile app, and through 300+ UK stores.
roleSummary: The role leads strategic and operational change in a distribution centre, improving performance, efficiency, safety, cost, and customer experience.
headlineAttraction: Lead meaningful transformation in a major distribution centre for a well-known fashion brand.
rolePurpose: To bridge strategy and day-to-day DC operations by delivering transformation that creates lasting improvements.
advertSummary: New Look is hiring a full-time Transformation Manager for its Longport distribution centre to lead change programmes, continuous improvement, and operational excellence across DC operations.
personalisedIntro: I’ve spent my career improving how busy operations work day to day, and I’m at my best when strategy has to become something practical on the floor. This role fits the way I work: structured change, clear measures, and real follow-through in a live operational setting.
whyThisRole: The work itself is the part that interests me most: taking change from plan to reality, improving performance, and making sure it sticks. I’ve worked in operations where service, control and improvement all had to move together, and I would bring that same discipline here.
coreResponsibilities: Own roadmaps, milestones, and benefits tracking
Drive continuous improvement using Lean/Six Sigma

Work with Operations, Engineering, IT, HR, Finance, and transformation teams

Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
Ability to influence without direct authority
Lean / Six Sigma certification (Green Belt or above)
Experience with WMS implementations or upgrades
Exposure to automation or mechanisation projects

Role is based in Longport ST5 9QD

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: I turn complex operations into steadier performance and lasting change.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’ve spent my career improving how busy operations work day to day, and I’m at my best when strategy has to become something practical on the floor. This role fits the way I work: structured change, clear measures, and real follow-through in a live operational setting.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: New Look stands out because it combines a well-known retail brand with a large UK store estate and digital channels, so the distribution centre has a direct impact on customer experience. I also like the emphasis on inclusion, development and a practical, flexible working culture.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: The work itself is the part that interests me most: taking change from plan to reality, improving performance, and making sure it sticks. I’ve worked in operations where service, control and improvement all had to move together, and I would bring that same discipline here.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Strong understanding of fast-paced operational environments and cross-functional change
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I led day-to-day operations in a specialist manufacturing setting during a scale-up phase, so I understand the pressure of improving performance while keeping delivery stable. That is close to the kind of pace and control this role needs.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the main operational credibility example for change in a live environment.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve led operations through growth while improving resilience and delivery discipline.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Change delivery, KPI improvement, and embedding better service outcomes
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I improved patient experience in a live NHS setting and turned around a poor external score through better operational grip and follow-through. That shows I can improve outcomes that matter and make the change visible in the numbers.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a headline proof point for measurable improvement and service impact.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I improved a live service score from 2.09 to 4.51 by tightening operational control.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Ride compliance and operator progression tracking
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Process control, compliance, and practical problem-solving in a safety-critical environment
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I built a simple tracking system to support compliance and operator progression in a safety-critical operation. That is relevant to a distribution centre where process control, safety and adoption all matter.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as supporting evidence for structured thinking, compliance and practical systems.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I built a tracking tool that made compliance and progression easier to manage.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I think this role needs someone who can take a transformation plan and make it work in the reality of a busy distribution centre. That means improving performance without losing control of safety, service or buy-in, and helping teams adopt better ways of working rather than just announcing them.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Deliver transformation in a live operational environment
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I led operations through a scale-up phase in specialist manufacturing, where I had to improve resilience, process discipline and delivery at the same time.
experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I can work in a fast-moving setting where change has to land without disrupting the operation.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Improve performance, service and quality through measurable change
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I improved patient experience scores significantly in NHS primary care by strengthening operational grip and follow-through.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role is clearly outcome-led, so I would bring a habit of tying change to visible performance improvement.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Use data and reporting to track benefits and priorities
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I built senior reporting across workflow, utilisation, payroll and income, and I worked with reconciliations across a large financial base.
experienceMappings.relevance: This supports the benefits tracking and KPI discipline the role calls for.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Lead cross-functional change and influence without direct authority
experienceMappings.myEvidence: At Experian I managed and supported a team through business-critical annual projects in a regulated environment, balancing service standards with delivery pressure.
experienceMappings.relevance: This is relevant to working with operations, IT, finance and other stakeholders around a shared plan.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Embed compliance and standard ways of working
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I created a user input system to monitor operating hours and support compliance and operator progression.
experienceMappings.relevance: That shows I can turn a practical problem into a usable control that people will actually work with.

focusAreasToBring.title: Practical change that sticks
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on making sure change is clear, owned and embedded, not just delivered on paper.

focusAreasToBring.title: Performance control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep attention on the measures that matter most: productivity, service, cost, quality and safety.

focusAreasToBring.title: Team adoption
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would spend time with leaders and frontline teams so new ways of working are understood, used and sustained.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring a mix of operational leadership, service improvement and structured change delivery across complex environments. I’ve worked in healthcare, manufacturing, leisure and regulated services, so I’m comfortable joining the dots between data, people and day-to-day execution. That gives me a strong base for a role that needs both transformation thinking and operational realism.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first months getting close to the operation, understanding where performance is being lost, and agreeing the few changes that will make the biggest difference. I would then focus on building a clear roadmap, supporting adoption, and making sure benefits are tracked and sustained after implementation.
companyHighlights: New Look operates across website, mobile app and more than 300 UK stores.
The role sits in a distribution centre and links site operations to customer experience.
The company highlights inclusion, development and a flexible working approach.
There are practical on-site benefits including parking and a subsidised canteen.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments that value practical ownership, clear measures and visible follow-through. The culture signals around customer focus, teamwork and continuous improvement fit the way I already operate.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the DC, learn the current performance picture, understand the main pain points, and build relationships with the leaders and teams who will need to carry the change.
I would help identify the highest-value improvement areas, agree how success will be measured, and turn that into a practical plan with milestones, owners and benefits tracking.
I would focus on workshops, coaching and follow-up so changes are being used consistently, with early checks on whether the expected gains in service, cost, quality or safety are showing through.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring a steady, practical approach to transformation and a clear focus on making improvements real, measurable and sustainable.
closingProofPoints: I improved a live NHS service score from 2.09 to 4.51.
I led operations through a manufacturing scale-up while strengthening resilience and discipline.
I built a compliance-tracking system in a safety-critical environment.
personalisedContent.coverLetter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to apply for the Transformation Manager role at New Look.

I have spent my career improving how complex operations perform in practice, not just on paper. In my current role in NHS primary care, I lead operational performance and transformation across clinical, administrative and patient-facing functions, and I have delivered measurable improvement in service experience, financial control and reporting. Before that, I led operations in specialist manufacturing during a scale-up phase, and earlier in my career I worked across large, live operational environments at Merlin Entertainments and Experian.

What draws me to this role is the combination of structured change, operational discipline and the need to make improvements stick. I have worked in environments where performance depends on clear measures, strong stakeholder engagement and careful follow-through, and I would bring that same approach to a distribution centre setting. I am comfortable working across teams, using data to shape priorities, and turning improvement ideas into practical changes that people can adopt.

I would welcome the chance to bring that experience to New Look and help deliver improvements that are sustainable, visible and useful to the operation.

Yours sincerely,

Ben Howard
contentNotes: The advert does not confirm Lean/Six Sigma certification or WMS/automation project experience in Ben’s CV, so I have not claimed those directly.
I have used the strongest available operational and transformation evidence from healthcare, manufacturing, leisure and regulated services to support a DC change role.
Company highlights are limited to facts stated in the advert and job post text.
